question:
TASK 2 (Aggregation):
Design a "Library" class that has a collection of "Book" objects. The "Library" class should allow adding books and displaying the details of all books in the library.
Instructions:
- Create a class "Book" with attributes "title", "author", and "publicationYear".
- Create a class "Library" that has a private attribute, a vector of type "Book".
- Implement methods "addBook()" and "displayBooks()" in the "Library" class.

test_cases.expected:
Library Books:
Title: 1984, Author: George Orwell, Year: 1949
Title: To Kill a Mockingbird, Author: Harper Lee, Year: 1960

errors: Poor parameter naming in Book constructor (t, a, y instead of descriptive names)
Inefficient pass by value in addBook method instead of const reference
Unnecessary copying of Book objects in displayBooks loop
Could use range-based for loop for better readability
Magic strings in main without explanation
